Patch Set 4:

Hello Kevin, thanks for the patch. 
We try to have logs also to STDOUT in order to simplify logs collection. 

Do you think it would be feasible to add them in this particular merge request?

Also, I know that « logback » allows part of configuration to be done via variables. 
Is it possible here? If yes it would be awesome to be able to set the log level via an environment variable (LOG_LEVEL for example with default to INFO).  
Doing that would help to simplify debugging when there are some issues.

Patch Set 4:

And sorry if I merged previous proposition that was not ready!

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> And sorry if I merged previous proposition that was not ready!

Something with the corporate proxy I am behind changed so one day I had difficulty pushing my changes.

I don't know if my update failed to push and I forgot or what :-(

I lost track of it and didn't mark it as work in progress so the error was truly mine.

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> Hello Kevin, thanks for the patch. 
> We try to have logs also to STDOUT in order to simplify logs collection. 
> 
> Do you think it would be feasible to add them in this particular merge request?
> 
> Also, I know that « logback » allows part of configuration to be done via variables. 
> Is it possible here? If yes it would be awesome to be able to set the log level via an environment variable (LOG_LEVEL for example with default to INFO).  
> Doing that would help to simplify debugging when there are some issues.

Log4j2 has a ConsoleAppender. "the ConsoleAppender writes its output to either System.out or System.err with System.out being the default target." https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/manual/appenders.html

It appears I broke something in my previous PR so I would really like to fix it with this one. For this reason further changes should be separate.

Should we stop logging to files altogether or log to both files and System.out? Alternatively a subset of logs could go to System.out... If everything goes to System.out how are things organized exactly? For example audit logs only go into audit.log today. Metric logs only go into metric.log. Stack traces are found in debug.log. Is this same separation available in the newer approach?

log4j2 does allow  this format  	
System environment variables. The formats are ${env:ENV_NAME} and ${env:ENV_NAME:-default_value}.
https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/manual/configuration.html

Just to be clear we code against slf4j and at runtime we have pax ops4j logging which has some bridge to log4j2.

This logging configuration file is similar to, but not identical with a log4j2.properties file.

If you change the environment variable I am not sure whether this takes affect immediately or how it behaves. Such things would need to be tested.

The way we change logging levels today is through the Karaf shell. This is done at runtime and requires no reboot.

Anytime the logging file itself changes it is re-loaded and it takes effect at runtime. This can be used as an alternative to the Karaf shell.

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> > Patch Set 4:
> > 
> > Hello Kevin, thanks for the patch. 
> > We try to have logs also to STDOUT in order to simplify logs collection. 
> > 
> > Do you think it would be feasible to add them in this particular merge request?
> > 
> > Also, I know that « logback » allows part of configuration to be done via variables. 
> > Is it possible here? If yes it would be awesome to be able to set the log level via an environment variable (LOG_LEVEL for example with default to INFO).  
> > Doing that would help to simplify debugging when there are some issues.
> 
> Log4j2 has a ConsoleAppender. "the ConsoleAppender writes its output to either System.out or System.err with System.out being the default target." https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/manual/appenders.html
> 
> It appears I broke something in my previous PR so I would really like to fix it with this one. For this reason further changes should be separate.

Ok, so let's merge this one !

> 
> Should we stop logging to files altogether or log to both files and System.out? Alternatively a subset of logs could go to System.out... If everything goes to System.out how are things organized exactly? For example audit logs only go into audit.log today. Metric logs only go into metric.log. Stack traces are found in debug.log. Is this same separation available in the newer approach?
> 

No, I was thinking putting "relevant" logs in STDOUT (everything above INFO for example) but still keeping the file in parallel as some may still want to use them.
After, based on my "experience", I've seen that usually only one log file is really fed (or maybe configuration wasn't done well)


> log4j2 does allow  this format  	
> System environment variables. The formats are ${env:ENV_NAME} and ${env:ENV_NAME:-default_value}.
> https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/manual/configuration.html
> 
> Just to be clear we code against slf4j and at runtime we have pax ops4j logging which has some bridge to log4j2.
> 
> This logging configuration file is similar to, but not identical with a log4j2.properties file.
> 
> If you change the environment variable I am not sure whether this takes affect immediately or how it behaves. Such things would need to be tested.
> 
> The way we change logging levels today is through the Karaf shell. This is done at runtime and requires no reboot.
> 
> Anytime the logging file itself changes it is re-loaded and it takes effect at runtime. This can be used as an alternative to the Karaf shell.

I'm not (at all) a JAVA expert so I trust what you say 😊
For the reboot or not, having the env variable would allow to be consistent accross all ONAP components (anyone would know that if they want DEBUG level on such component, they just have to modify it on deployment, the pod restart with the desired value)
For experts of a particular component, they would still be able to use the best way like karaf shell in yODL case.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+2

and sorry again, is it OK to merge now?

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4: Code-Review+2
> 
> and sorry again, is it OK to merge now?

Yes please merge.

> For experts of a particular component, they would still be able to use the best way like karaf shell in yODL case.

Is there some additional reading I can do about the switch to std out? This is a pretty common thing in the industry, but I had some implementation specific questions.

I am willing to help in whatever capacity I can. I have wrestled logging long enough that I can tell it to do whatever somebody wants if I fully understand it in English first.
Change has been successfully merged by Sylvain Desbureaux
